questions.question: What title did Prince Salim take after becoming emperor?
questions.explanation: Prince Salim took the title Jahangir, meaning 'Conqueror of the World' in Persian, after ascending the Mughal throne in 1605.

questions.question: Who led the rebellion against Jahangir soon after his accession?
questions.explanation: Prince Khusrau, Jahangir's son, rebelled in 1606 in Lahore with support from powerful nobles. The rebellion was personally suppressed by Jahangir.

questions.question: Which Sikh Guru was executed by Jahangir for helping Prince Khusrau?
questions.explanation: The fifth Sikh Guru, Arjan Dev, had given shelter to Prince Khusrau at Taran Taran and was executed by Jahangir. This sowed the seeds of the long struggle between Mughals and Sikhs.

questions.question: Who led the decisive Mughal campaign against Rana Amar Singh of Mewar in 1613-14?
questions.explanation: Khurram (Jahangir's third son, later Shah Jahan) led the decisive campaign in 1613-14 that forced Rana Amar Singh to submit to the Mughals in 1615.

questions.question: Who was the Abyssinian Prime Minister who kept Ahmadnagar independent from the Mughals?
questions.explanation: Malik Ambar, the Abyssinian Prime Minister of Ahmadnagar, successfully resisted multiple Mughal expeditions from 1608 onwards and kept Ahmadnagar outside Mughal control.

questions.question: When did the East India Company receive its royal charter from Queen Elizabeth I?
questions.explanation: The East India Company received its royal charter from Queen Elizabeth I on December 31, 1600, giving it the right to trade in the East.

questions.question: Who was sent by Emperor James I of England to Jahangir's court in 1615?
questions.explanation: Sir Thomas Roe was sent by Emperor James I of England in 1615 to secure more trading concessions. He was very diplomatic and secured a royal charter for the Company to trade freely in the whole Mughal territory.

questions.question: What does the title 'Nur Jahan' mean?
questions.explanation: Jahangir gave his wife the title 'Nur Jahan' which means 'Light of the World'. He met her in the palace bazaar in 1611 and proposed marriage.

questions.question: From whom did Nur Jahan rescue Jahangir in 1626?
questions.explanation: Nur Jahan was known for her shrewd planning and physical strength. She rescued Jahangir from the clutches of rebel leader Mahabat Khan in 1626.

questions.question: What was the title taken by Prince Khurram after becoming emperor?
questions.explanation: Prince Khurram took the title Shah Jahan meaning 'Ruler of the World' after defeating his brothers and ascending the throne following Jahangir's death in 1627.

questions.question: Shah Jahan's mother was a princess from which Rajput dynasty?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's mother was a Rathore princess, the daughter of the Rajput ruler of Marwar (Jodhpur).

questions.question: Which kingdom did Shah Jahan annex in the Deccan in 1632?
questions.explanation: In 1632, Shah Jahan started a military campaign against Ahmadnagar and annexed it. He then ordered Bijapur and Golconda to submit.

questions.question: Who was appointed Governor of Bengal to deal with the Portuguese?
questions.explanation: In 1631, Shah Jahan appointed Qasim Khan as Governor of Bengal and gave him authority to teach the Portuguese a lesson for converting orphaned children to Christianity.
questions.source_section: Shah Jahan - Policy Towards the Portuguese

questions.question: The Peacock Throne contained which famous diamond?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's jewel-encrusted Peacock Throne contained the famous 186-carat Koh-i-Noor diamond, along with hundreds of rubies, emeralds, and pearls.

questions.question: Who stole the Peacock Throne from Delhi in 1739?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah of Persia sacked Delhi in 1739 and stole the Peacock Throne along with other treasures.

questions.question: Who was nominated by Shah Jahan as his heir?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan nominated his eldest son Dara Shikoh as his heir, but there was no system of primogeniture. Aurangzeb eventually defeated Dara and seized the throne.

questions.question: For how many years did Shah Jahan remain imprisoned at Agra Fort?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan remained in prison for eight years at Agra Fort after being imprisoned by his son Aurangzeb. He died there in 1666.

questions.question: Where was Guru Tegh Bahadur executed?
questions.explanation: Guru Tegh Bahadur, the ninth Sikh Guru, was executed in December 1675 in Chandni Chowk, Delhi, after refusing to embrace Islam on Aurangzeb's orders.

questions.question: When was Bijapur annexed by Aurangzeb?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb annexed Bijapur in 1686 and Golconda in 1687 during his long Deccan campaign.

questions.question: Which treaty did Shivaji sign in 1665?
questions.explanation: Shivaji signed the Treaty of Purandhar in 1665 after being besieged by Raja Jai Singh. He had to surrender 23 of his 35 forts and keep the remaining 12.
questions.source_section: Rise of the Marathas and Chhatrapati Shivaji

questions.question: In which year did Shivaji crown himself Maratha king at Raigarh?
questions.explanation: In 1674, Shivaji made Raigarh his capital and crowned himself Maratha king, taking the title of Chhatrapati.
questions.source_section: Rise of the Marathas and Chhatrapati Shivaji

questions.question: Which method of decoration was extensively used in Shah Jahan's monuments?
questions.explanation: Pietra dura (also called parelinkari) involved inlaying semi-precious stones into marble to create floral designs. This was the signature decorative method of Shah Jahan's architecture.

questions.question: Who built the tomb of Itimad-ud-Daula?
questions.explanation: The tomb of Itimad-ud-Daula (Nur Jahan's father) was built by Nur Jahan during Jahangir's reign.

questions.question: Who wrote the Padshahnama?
questions.explanation: Abdul Hamid Lahori wrote the Padshahnama during Shah Jahan's reign.

questions.question: Which Bijapur general was killed by Shivaji in 1659?
questions.explanation: In 1659, the Sultan of Bijapur sent general Afzal Khan to capture Shivaji, but Shivaji killed him instead.
questions.source_section: Rise of the Marathas and Chhatrapati Shivaji
